cwe: CWE-312 - Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information

threat.title: JWT Secret Exposure via Environment Variables in Express.js
threat.description: When JWT signing secrets are stored in .env files committed to version control, attackers can forge authentication tokens and impersonate any user, including administrators. This is one of the most common authentication bypass vulnerabilities in Node.js applications.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker discovers .env file in public GitHub repository or through directory traversal
2. Extracts JWT_SECRET value (e.g., 'mysecretkey123')
3. Uses the secret to sign malicious JWT tokens with admin claims
4. Sends forged token in Authorization header
5. Gains unrestricted admin access to the application
6. Exfiltrates sensitive data or modifies critical resources
prerequisites: JWT secret stored in .env file or environment variables
Repository is public OR attacker has access via leak/breach
Application uses HS256 (HMAC) symmetric signing algorithm
No additional token validation (e.g., IP binding, refresh tokens)

cve_references.description: Hardcoded credentials in Uber's GitHub repositories led to AWS access

real_world_breaches.impact: 57 million users and 600,000 drivers compromised; $148 million settlement
real_world_breaches.root_cause: AWS credentials hardcoded in GitHub repository, leading to S3 bucket access

real_world_breaches.impact: Over 10 million secrets detected in public GitHub commits in 2022
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Developers accidentally committing .env files and API keys

bug_bounty_reports.summary: JWT secret exposure in .env file allowing full account takeover

security_research.title: How to Secure Node.js Applications: JWT Secret Management
security_research.key_finding: JWT secret exposure is among top 3 authentication vulnerabilities in Node.js apps

mitigations.title: Use Azure Key Vault for Secret Management
mitigations.description: Store JWT secrets in Azure Key Vault and retrieve them at runtime using Managed Identity. This ensures secrets are never stored in code or configuration files.
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
validation_steps: Verify .env file does not contain JWT_SECRET
Confirm Key Vault URL is set in environment
Test secret rotation without application restart
Verify Managed Identity has 'Get' permission on secrets

mitigations.title: Switch to Asymmetric RS256 JWT Signing
mitigations.description: Use RS256 (RSA public/private key pairs) instead of HS256 (shared secret). This allows public key distribution for verification while keeping the private signing key secure.
iso27001_controls: A.10.1.1 - Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Store private key in Azure Key Vault or secure file system
Distribute public key to all services needing token verification
Update JWT middleware to use RS256 algorithm

mitigations.title: Implement Pre-Commit Secret Scanning with Gitleaks
mitigations.description: Prevent secrets from being committed to version control using automated pre-commit hooks and CI/CD scanning.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Install Gitleaks
brew install gitleaks  # macOS
# OR
curl -sSfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/master/scripts/install.sh | sh

# Create pre-commit hook
cat > .git/hooks/pre-commit << 'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
echo "Running Gitleaks secret scan..."
gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "❌ Secret detected! Commit blocked."
    echo "Remove the secret and try again."
    exit 1
fi
EOF

chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Configure .gitleaks.toml
cat > .gitleaks.toml << 'EOF'
title = "Ansvar Gitleaks Configuration"

[extend]
path = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/master/config/gitleaks.toml"

# Custom rule for JWT secrets
[[rules]]
id = "jwt-secret"
description = "Detected JWT secret in code"
regex = '''(?i)(jwt[_-]?secret|jwt[_-]?key|secret[_-]?key)\s*[=:]\s*['"][a-zA-Z0-9-_\.]{20,}['"]'''

[[rules]]
id = "env-file"
description = "Detected .env file with secrets"
path = '''\.env$'''
EOF

# Add GitHub Actions workflow
mkdir -p .github/workflows
cat > .github/workflows/gitleaks.yml << 'EOF'
name: Gitleaks Secret Scanning

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main, develop ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main, develop ]

jobs:
  scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      
      - name: Run Gitleaks
        uses: gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GITLEAKS_LICENSE: ${{ secrets.GITLEAKS_LICENSE }} # For enterprise features
EOF

nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
validation_steps: Install Gitleaks locally and in CI/CD
Test by adding dummy secret (should block commit)
Verify GitHub Actions workflow runs on push
Check GitHub Security tab for findings

indicators: Unauthorized admin access from unexpected IP addresses
JWT tokens with user claims that don't match database records
Multiple concurrent sessions for a single user with different IPs
JWT tokens with unusually long expiration times
Tokens issued before user creation date

log_queries.query:
// Detect admin access from suspicious IPs
SigninLogs
| where ResultType == "0" // Success
| where UserPrincipalName contains "admin"
| where IPAddress !in (dynamic(["trusted_ip_1", "trusted_ip_2"]))
| summarize LoginCount = count() by UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where LoginCount > 5
| order by LoginCount desc
event.category: "authentication" AND event.outcome: "success" AND user.roles: "admin" AND NOT source.ip: (10.0.0.0/8 OR 172.16.0.0/12)

index=app_logs sourcetype=express_access
| where status=200 AND user_role="admin" AND NOT cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8", client_ip)
| stats count by user_id, client_ip, _time
| where count > 10

tools.name: GitGuardian
tools.usage: Monitors all commits in real-time for exposed secrets
